transparent.question: Riya's grandmother uses a frosted glass door for her bathroom. Why is frosted glass a good choice for this?
options: Because it is transparent and people can see through
Because it is translucent — lets light in but blocks clear view
Because it is opaque and blocks all light
Because it is shiny and reflects light
transparent.hint: Think about privacy and light at the same time.

transparent.question: Which of these lists has ALL transparent objects?
options: Glass window, clear water, air
Frosted glass, butter paper, thin cloth
Wood, metal sheet, thick book
Clear glass, butter paper, iron sheet
transparent.hint: Transparent objects let you see through them completely clearly.

transparent.question: Aman puts his hand behind a thick wooden plank and shines a torch from the front. What will happen?
options: His hand will be visible through the plank
His hand will appear blurry through the plank
No light will pass through — his hand will not be visible
A rainbow will form

shadows.question: Deepa holds a transparent glass in sunlight. Will it form a clear shadow on the ground?
options: Yes, a very dark clear shadow
No, transparent objects don't block light so no clear shadow forms
Yes, but only at night
Only if the glass is coloured
shadows.hint: Think about what a transparent object does with light.

shadows.question: Three things are needed for a shadow to form. Which option has ALL three correctly?
options: Mirror, object, water
Light source, opaque object, screen
Lens, colour, wall
Sun, window, camera
shadows.hint: A shadow needs something to block the light, something to provide light, and somewhere to fall.

shadows.question: Priya's shadow falls behind her as she walks towards the school gate. Where is the sun?
options: Behind her (the sun is at her back)
In front of her
Directly above her head
Below the horizon
shadows.hint: Shadows always fall on the opposite side from the light source.

shadow_properties.question: Meena uses a red torch and shines it on a blue ball. What colour will the shadow be?
options: Red
Blue
Purple
Black
shadow_properties.hint: The colour of a shadow does not depend on the object or light colour.

shadow_properties.question: Rohan's shadow in the morning is very long. By afternoon it became very short. What caused this change?
options: Rohan grew taller
The ground changed shape
The sun's position changed — it moved higher in the sky
The shadow moved on its own
shadow_properties.hint: Think about how the angle of the sun changes throughout the day.

shadow_properties.question: Arjun holds a circular coin in front of a torch. In which position will the shadow look like a straight line (not a circle)?
options: When the coin faces the torch directly
When the coin is tilted sideways so its edge faces the torch
When the coin is placed flat on the ground
The shadow is always circular
shadow_properties.hint: Think about what the thin edge of a coin looks like.

pinhole.question: Kavya makes a pinhole camera and points it at a tree. Where will the image of the tree appear and how will it look?
options: On the screen, upright and same size
On the screen, inverted (upside down)
On the pinhole side of the box
The image will float in the air
pinhole.hint: Light from the top of the tree passes through the hole to the bottom of the screen.

pinhole.question: Why should the inside of a pinhole camera be painted black?
options: Black paint makes the box stronger
To prevent reflections inside the box that would blur the image
Black is the traditional colour for cameras
To make the pinhole appear bigger
pinhole.hint: Think about what happens to light when it reflects off white or shiny surfaces inside.

pinhole.question: Sunita notices that when she moves her pinhole camera closer to the object, the image becomes larger. This proves that:
options: Closer objects appear smaller in pinhole cameras
The size of the image depends on distance from the object
Pinhole cameras only work from far away
The hole gets bigger when closer
pinhole.hint: The angle of rays entering the hole changes with distance.

reflection.question: Vikram looks at a rough brick wall. He cannot see his reflection. Why?
options: Walls absorb all light
Walls don't reflect light at all
Rough surfaces cause irregular/diffuse reflection — no clear image forms
Vikram's eyes are not working properly
reflection.hint: Smooth vs rough surfaces reflect light very differently.

reflection.question: A solar cooker uses curved mirrors to cook food. How does this work?
options: Mirrors generate heat on their own
Curved mirrors reflect sunlight and focus it onto a small area, creating heat
Mirrors absorb sunlight and become hot
Mirrors filter out cold light rays
reflection.hint: Reflection can be directed — think about where reflected rays would go with a curved mirror.

reflection.question: Aman writes the word 'AMBULANCE' backwards on the front of a vehicle. Why?
options: It is a mistake
So it looks correct in the rear-view mirror of vehicles ahead
Backwards writing is easier to read
To confuse people
reflection.hint: A mirror reverses writing — so reversed writing looks normal in a mirror.

day_night.question: If Earth did NOT rotate on its axis, what would happen?
options: We would have longer days only
One side of Earth would always be in daylight and the other always in darkness
Day and night would alternate faster
The sun would rise in the West
day_night.hint: Day and night happen BECAUSE Earth rotates.

day_night.question: Why does the Sun appear to rise in the East every morning?
options: The Sun moves from East to West
Earth rotates from West to East, making the Sun appear to move East to West
The Sun is fixed and Earth is also fixed
The Moon pushes the Sun upward
day_night.hint: Remember — the Sun doesn't actually move. It's Earth that moves.

day_night.question: Deepa is in Delhi. Her pen pal Lily is in London. It is 2 PM in Delhi. Will it be daytime or nighttime in London?
options: It will be 2 PM in London too
It will be nighttime in London
It will be early morning in London (about 8:30 AM)
London and Delhi always have the same time
day_night.hint: London is west of Delhi — about 5.5 hours behind Indian Standard Time.
